table_introductions.table_introduction: Sales document header table that captures order type, sales area, sold-to party, dates, and currency. It is the primary header for sales order reporting.
Sales document item table containing material, quantity, plant, pricing relevance, and item controls. It is used for item-level order analysis.
Schedule line table for sales items that stores confirmed quantities and delivery dates. It supports ATP and delivery planning analysis.
Document flow table linking preceding and subsequent SD documents. It enables end-to-end order-to-cash traceability.
Shipment document flow table linking shipment documents to deliveries and related SD documents. It supports shipment traceability.
Sales document business data table with incoterms, payment terms, and pricing dates. It enriches orders with commercial attributes.
Partner function table for sales documents with sold-to, ship-to, payer, and bill-to roles. It is used for partner and address analytics.
Header-level status table for sales documents. It summarizes overall processing status for delivery and billing.
Item-level status table for sales documents. It tracks detailed delivery and billing status per item.
Incomplete data log for sales documents and items. It is used to identify missing mandatory data.
Billing document header table with invoice-level dates, currency, and accounting data. It is central to billing analytics.
Billing document item table containing billed materials, quantities, values, and tax fields. It supports revenue and tax reporting.
Billing document flow and reversal reference table. It helps track cancellations and document relationships.
Sales partner data table for activities and related documents. It is used to analyze partner roles in SD processes.
Outbound delivery header table with shipping point, dates, and overall status. It supports logistics and fulfillment reporting.
Outbound delivery item table with material, quantity, batch, and storage location. It is used for delivery performance and inventory movements.
Shipment header table for transportation planning. It stores shipment dates, status, and route data.
Shipment item table linking deliveries to shipment documents. It enables shipment-level quantity reconciliation.
Shipment stage table storing legs, carriers, and stage dates. It is used for route and transit analysis.
Shipment item status table with packing and processing indicators. It supports shipment execution monitoring.
Shipment partner table that records carrier and partner assignments. It supports logistics partner reporting.
Handling unit header table describing packaging units. It is used for HU-level logistics analysis.
Handling unit item table linking materials and quantities to HUs. It supports packaging composition reporting.
Pricing communication header table for SD pricing. It provides header data used during condition determination.
Pricing communication item table for SD pricing. It provides item data used in condition calculation.
Pricing condition transaction table with calculated prices, discounts, and surcharges. It is used for margin analysis.
Pricing condition header table with condition type and validity. It groups condition records for pricing analysis.
Pricing condition item table with rates, scales, and calculation data. It is used for detailed pricing breakdowns.
Billing plan header table for periodic billing. It defines the plan structure for contracts.
Billing plan dates table for scheduled billing events. It supports billing schedule reporting.
Sales document type text table with language-dependent descriptions. It is used to label document types.
Sales item category text table for readable item category names. It supports UI and reporting labels.
Sales organization configuration table. It links sales orgs to company codes and control data.
Sales office configuration table. It defines sales office master data for reporting.
Sales organization text table with language-dependent names. It is used for descriptive reporting.
Sales office text table with language-dependent descriptions. It supports multi-language displays.
Distribution channel text table. It provides readable names for channel codes.
Delivery status definition table. It defines technical status codes for deliveries.
Delivery status text table with language-dependent labels. It supports readable status reporting.
Sales document type configuration table. It defines behavior for sales document categories.
Sales document type text table. It provides language-dependent descriptions.
Delivery type configuration table. It defines control data for delivery processing.
Delivery type text table with readable descriptions. It supports multilingual reporting.
Item category configuration table. It defines processing controls for item categories.
Item category text table. It provides language-dependent descriptions.
Item category determination table. It maps document type and item usage to item categories.
Item category determination text table. It supports readable configuration reporting.
Shipping point configuration table. It defines shipping points and related controls.
Shipping point text table. It provides language-dependent descriptions for shipping points.
Pricing procedure configuration table. It defines calculation schemas for SD pricing.
Pricing procedure text table. It provides language-dependent pricing procedure names.
Pricing procedure determination table. It maps sales area and customer to pricing procedures.
Pricing procedure determination text table. It provides readable descriptions for pricing determination.
Condition type configuration table. It defines condition types and processing controls.
Condition type text table. It provides language-dependent condition type descriptions.
Pricing condition records by sales org, distribution channel, customer, and material. It supports customer-material pricing.
Pricing condition records by sales org, distribution channel, and customer. It supports customer-specific pricing.
Pricing condition records by sales org, distribution channel, and material. It supports material pricing.
Pricing condition records by sales org, distribution channel, and material group. It supports group-based pricing.
Pricing condition records by sales org, distribution channel, customer, and material group. It supports customer group pricing.
Pricing condition records by sales org, distribution channel, and customer price group. It supports price group-based pricing.
Pricing condition records by sales org, distribution channel, customer price group, and material. It supports price group material pricing.
Pricing condition records by sales org, distribution channel, customer price group, and material group. It supports grouped pricing rules.
Pricing condition records by sales org, distribution channel, customer, and plant. It supports plant-specific customer pricing.
Pricing condition records by sales org, distribution channel, and material pricing group. It supports pricing group maintenance.
General customer master table with name, address, and classification fields. It is the core customer identity source.
Company-code-specific customer accounting data. It includes reconciliation account and payment terms.
Sales-area-specific customer master data. It stores pricing, shipping, and credit attributes.
Customer partner functions table defining sold-to, ship-to, bill-to, and payer roles. It supports partner analysis.
Customer bank details table. It stores bank keys and account data for payments.
Customer dunning data table. It stores dunning procedures and blocks per company code.
Customer credit management data per company code. It supports credit exposure reporting.
Customer contact person table. It stores names and contact details for business contacts.
Customer credit master table by credit control area. It stores credit limits and risk categories.
Customer credit account table with credit exposure by credit control area. It is used for credit checks.
Material master general data table with material number, type, and base unit. It is the core material identity source.
Material description table by language. It provides readable material names for reporting.
Alternative units of measure table with conversion factors. It supports quantity conversion and packaging analysis.
Sales-related material master data table. It includes sales unit and item category group settings.
Plant-level material master data table with MRP and procurement parameters. It drives planning and availability reporting.
Storage location stock table with unrestricted, blocked, and reserved quantities. It is used for inventory availability.
Batch master table with batch attributes and validity data. It supports batch traceability and quality analysis.
Batch master general data table. It stores batch-level attributes across plants.
Batch stock table by plant and storage location. It is used for batch-level inventory reporting.
Material valuation table with price control and valuation values. It supports cost and valuation reporting.
Material document header table with posting dates and document types. It provides movement header context.
Material document item table with movement type and quantity. It is the primary source for inventory movements.
Physical inventory document header table. It stores inventory count header data.
Physical inventory document item table. It stores count items and quantities.
Special stock quantities table by material and plant. It supports tracking of special stock categories.
Material document reversal table linking reversals to original postings. It supports audit of corrections.
Material to BOM assignment table. It links materials to BOM headers for explosion.
Material warehouse data table by warehouse number. It supports WM integration attributes.
Material storage type data table by WM storage type. It supports WM control and capacity planning.
Sales order stock table for special stock E. It tracks customer-specific stock quantities.
Vendor consignment stock table for special stock K. It supports consignment inventory reporting.
Special stock at vendor table for subcontracting stock. It supports external stock visibility.
Consignment stock table by material and vendor. It supports consignment valuation and reporting.
Forecasting data table for material planning. It stores parameters and forecast results.
Material consumption statistics table. It supports historical consumption analysis.
Plant definition table with plant name and valuation area link. It is used across logistics modules.
Storage location definition table with descriptions and control flags. It supports stock reporting by location.
Movement type attributes table for inventory management. It defines control indicators for postings.
Movement type configuration table. It defines movement type behavior and allowed postings.
Movement type text table. It provides language-dependent descriptions for movement types.
Movement type control table for special stock and account assignment. It supports IM configuration reporting.
Material type configuration table. It defines material type control settings.
Material type text table with language-dependent descriptions. It supports readable reporting.
Material group configuration table. It defines material group codes used for classification.
Material group text table. It provides language-dependent material group names.
Purchasing document header table for purchase orders and contracts. It stores vendor, org, and document type data.
Purchasing document item table with material, quantity, and price. It supports PO line analysis.
Purchasing schedule line table with delivery dates and quantities. It supports delivery performance analysis.
Purchasing document history table capturing GR and IR postings. It supports procurement cycle reconciliation.
Purchase requisition table for internal demand. It is used to analyze requisition processing.
Purchasing info record general data table. It links vendor and material for sourcing.
Purchasing info record org data table with pricing and delivery tolerances. It supports vendor term analysis.
Account assignment table for purchasing items. It links PO items to cost objects.
Purchasing confirmations table for acknowledgments and ASN. It supports vendor performance monitoring.
Partner functions for purchasing documents. It stores partner roles for procurement.
Source list table for material sourcing. It enforces approved vendor selection.
Service line items table for service procurement. It captures service details and values.
Service entry sheet header table. It tracks service confirmations and approvals.
Vendor master general data table with identity and address. It is the core vendor master source.
Vendor company-code data table with reconciliation account and payment terms. It supports AP processing.
Vendor purchasing organization data table. It stores procurement parameters and status.
Vendor bank details table. It stores bank accounts for vendor payments.
Vendor dunning data table. It stores dunning procedures and blocks for vendors.
Logistics invoice verification header table. It stores vendor invoice headers for MM.
Logistics invoice verification item table. It stores invoice items for MM invoices.
Purchasing group configuration table. It defines purchasing group codes and descriptions.
Purchasing organization configuration table. It defines purchasing orgs for procurement.
Purchasing organization text table. It provides language-dependent descriptions.
Purchasing group text table. It provides readable names for purchasing groups.
Plant and purchasing organization assignments. It supports procurement organizational reporting.
Purchase requisition document type configuration. It defines processing controls for PRs.
Purchase order document type configuration. It defines PO controls and number ranges.
Warehouse quant table with stock at bin level. It is used for WM stock reporting.
Transfer order header table. It stores TO header data for warehouse movements.
Transfer order item table. It stores line-level movements for TOs.
Storage bin master table with bin attributes and capacity. It supports bin utilization reporting.
Warehouse number definition table. It stores warehouse configuration attributes.
Transfer order confirmation header table. It supports confirmation and audit reporting.
Transfer order confirmation item table. It stores confirmed quantities per TO item.
Accounting document header table with company code, dates, and document type. It is the primary FI header table.
Accounting document line item table with G/L, customer, and vendor postings. It provides transaction-level detail.
Tax line item table for FI documents. It stores tax base and tax amount values.
Withholding tax line item table. It supports withholding tax reporting and reconciliation.
Open G/L line items table. It tracks uncleared postings for reconciliation.
Cleared G/L line items table. It stores settled postings for audit and analysis.
Open customer items table. It supports AR aging and open item reporting.
Cleared customer items table. It supports cleared AR analysis.
Open vendor items table. It supports AP aging and open item reporting.
Cleared vendor items table. It supports cleared AP analysis.
Secondary index for FI line items. It supports reporting and selection performance.
G/L account master at chart of accounts level. It defines account numbers and groups.
G/L account master at company code level. It stores posting controls and currencies.
G/L account text table by language. It provides readable account names.
Company code definition table. It defines legal entities for accounting.
Company code text table. It provides language-dependent descriptions.
Company code clearing settings table. It stores clearing configuration per company.
Valuation area table. It assigns valuation area keys to plants or company codes.
Document type configuration table. It defines FI document types and controls.
Posting key configuration table. It defines debit/credit indicators and account types.
Fiscal year variant table. It defines the fiscal year structure.
Posting period table. It controls open posting periods per variant.
Fiscal year variant text table. It provides readable variant names.
Classic G/L totals table. It stores aggregated balances by period.
New G/L line items table with additional dimensions. It supports segment reporting.
New G/L totals table by period and dimension. It supports fast financial reporting.
Profit center line item table. It supports profit center accounting analysis.
Profit center totals table. It stores period balances by profit center.
Payment terms configuration table. It defines cash discount and due date rules.
Payment terms text and details table. It stores language-dependent descriptions and components.
Payment run header table. It stores run-level payment information.
Payment run item table. It stores individual payment items for runs.
Tax code configuration table. It defines tax codes and tax calculation settings.
Automatic account determination table. It maps transaction keys to G/L accounts.
Asset master record table with asset class and description. It is the core asset identity source.
Asset value table by depreciation area and fiscal year. It stores acquisition and depreciation values.
Asset accounting document header table. It stores posting metadata for asset documents.
Asset accounting line item table. It stores detailed postings per asset.
Asset depreciation terms table. It stores depreciation keys and useful life.
Asset allocation table for cost center and profit center. It supports asset assignment reporting.
Asset planned depreciation table. It stores planned depreciation values by period.
Asset line items for retirements and transfers. It supports asset movement reporting.
CO document header table with controlling area and posting data. It provides CO header context.
CO line item table with cost element and object assignments. It is used for detailed cost analysis.
CO totals table for external postings by period. It supports cost center totals reporting.
CO totals table for internal allocations by period. It supports internal cost analysis.
CO line item table for controlling postings. It stores detailed CO document line items.
Cost element master at chart of accounts level. It defines primary and secondary cost elements.
Cost element master at controlling area level. It stores validity and attributes.
Cost center master table. It stores organizational cost object definitions.
Cost center text table with language-dependent names. It supports readable reporting.
Activity type master table. It defines activity types used in allocations.
Controlling area configuration table. It defines controlling area settings and currency.
Cost element category table. It defines cost element groupings.
Profit center master table. It stores profit center definitions and assignments.
Profit center text table by language. It provides readable profit center names.
Order master table for internal, production, and maintenance orders. It stores order type and controlling data.
Project definition master table. It stores project IDs and organizational attributes.
WBS element master table. It stores hierarchical project structures and statuses.
WBS hierarchy table. It stores parent-child relationships for WBS elements.
Project dates table. It stores key dates for project planning.
Project status table. It stores status information for PS objects.
Project network header table. It stores network numbers and statuses.
Production order header table. It stores order dates, status, and material data.
Production order item table. It stores material and quantity details per order.
Production order operation table. It stores operation steps and work center references.
Operation values table with planned and actual times. It supports capacity and cost analysis.
Confirmation table for production orders. It captures yields, scrap, and time confirmations.
Order confirmations table for production and maintenance orders. It stores confirmation details and posting data.
Planned order table generated by MRP. It supports planning and scheduling analysis.
Routing header table. It defines routing groups and validity dates.
Routing operation table with operation details. It supports routing analysis.
Routing sequence table. It defines operation sequences for routings.
Routing operation text table. It provides descriptive texts for operations.
Task list assignment table linking materials to routings. It supports routing selection.
Reservation table for components and requirements. It supports component consumption analysis.
BOM header table defining BOM structures. It is used for BOM explosion reporting.
BOM item table with component details and quantities. It supports component usage analysis.
Work center header table with capacities and control data. It supports capacity reporting.
Work center capacity table. It stores capacity definitions and validity.
Work center cost center assignment table. It links work centers to cost centers.
Work center text table. It provides language-dependent work center descriptions.
Production order header history table. It supports audit and change analysis.
MRP header table. It stores planning run and header data for MRP elements.
MRP item table. It stores MRP elements and requirements details.
Independent requirements header table. It stores planned independent requirements by material.
Independent requirements item table. It stores schedule lines for requirements.
Equipment master table with equipment number, type, and plant. It is the core equipment reference.
Equipment text table with language-dependent descriptions. It supports readable equipment reporting.
Functional location master table with hierarchy data. It supports asset structure analysis.
Maintenance order header table for PM orders. It links orders to equipment and functional locations.
Location and account assignment table for technical objects. It supports cost assignment and location reporting.
Functional location category table. It defines types and control attributes.
PM partner assignment table for technical objects. It tracks responsible partners and roles.
Equipment time segment table. It stores validity and assignment segments.

Object table for Plant Maintenance that stores equipment and functional location objects with object numbers, status codes, and technical attributes. It links to equipment and functional location master data for object-based reporting.

Serial number history table that tracks material document movements linked to objects. It records inventory postings, movement types, and quantities for serialized equipment and materials in Plant Maintenance.
Maintenance notification header table. It stores PM notification metadata.
Maintenance notification item table. It stores defect items for notifications.
Maintenance notification cause table. It stores root cause details.
Maintenance plan header table. It defines time-based and performance-based plans.
Maintenance plan items table. It stores individual plan items and task lists.
Maintenance history table. It stores historical maintenance completion data.
Inspection lot header table with plant and status data. It is the core QM inspection table.
Inspection results statistics table. It stores aggregated results for characteristics.
Usage decision table for inspection lots. It captures final inspection outcomes.
Inspection plan header table. It defines inspection plan identity and validity.
Inspection plan item table. It stores inspection characteristics and methods.
Inspection characteristic results table. It stores detailed results and valuation.
Inspection characteristic text table. It provides language-dependent descriptions.
Inspection characteristic group table. It supports grouping and reporting.
Quality info record table for vendor-material combinations. It supports procurement quality checks.
QM data in material master table. It stores QM control indicators per material.
Quality notification tasks table. It stores tasks assigned to QM notifications.
Quality notification activities table. It stores activities recorded for QM notifications.
Quality notification defects table. It stores defect data for QM notifications.
HR actions infotype table with employee status changes. It records lifecycle events.
Organizational assignment infotype table. It links employees to org units and cost centers.
Personal data infotype table with names and demographics. It is used for employee master reporting.
Payroll status infotype table. It stores payroll control data for employees.
HR infotype table for personal data. It stores additional personal information.
Address infotype table with employee address history. It supports contact reporting.
Planned working time infotype table. It stores work schedule and time management data.
Basic pay infotype table with wage types and amounts. It supports compensation analysis.
HR infotype table for bank details. It stores employee bank account data.
Recurring payments and deductions infotype table. It supports payroll recurring entries.
Additional payments infotype table. It stores one-time payment data.
HR infotype table for contract elements. It stores contract details and dates.
Family/related persons infotype table. It supports dependent reporting.
HR infotype table for education. It stores education and qualification records.
Reference data infotype table. It stores references for HR master data.
HR infotype table for date specifications. It stores key employee dates.
Communication infotype table with email and user IDs. It supports identity linkage.
HR infotype table for personal IDs. It stores identity documents and ID numbers.
Work relationship infotype table. It stores contract and relationship data.
Actions and measures infotype table. It supports personnel action tracking.
Absence infotype table. It stores leave and absence records.
Attendance infotype table. It stores attendance records.
HR infotype table for absence quotas. It stores leave quota entitlements.
Employee remuneration infotype table. It stores time-based remuneration data.
Time events infotype table. It stores clock-in and clock-out events.
Substitutions infotype table. It stores substitution and replacement data.
HR infotype table for time data. It stores time evaluation and balance details.
HR action infotype table for personnel actions. It stores action control data.
Personnel area configuration table. It defines personnel area codes and names.
Personnel subarea configuration table. It defines subareas and groupings.
Employee group configuration table. It defines employee group codes.
Employee subgroup configuration table. It defines subgroup codes.
Pay scale table. It defines pay scale types and areas.
Pay scale area text table. It provides readable pay scale names.
Pay scale group table. It defines pay scale groups.
Wage type table with wage type definitions. It supports payroll reporting.
Wage type text table by language. It provides readable wage type descriptions.
Organizational unit text table. It provides language-dependent org unit names.
Position text table by language. It supports org structure reporting.
Time management control table. It stores parameters for time evaluation.
Change document header table. It records who changed what and when.
Change document item table with field-level changes. It supports audit trails.
Data dictionary table metadata table. It describes technical attributes of tables.
Data dictionary table text table. It provides language-dependent table descriptions.
Data dictionary field metadata table. It defines field data types and keys.
Data dictionary field text table. It provides field descriptions by language.
Data dictionary data elements table. It defines semantic field attributes.
Data element text table. It provides semantic descriptions of fields.
Data dictionary domains table. It defines domain data types and lengths.
Data dictionary domain value text table. It provides value descriptions by language.
Transaction code table. It defines transaction codes and program assignments.
Transaction code text table. It maps T-codes to descriptions.
Variant configuration table. It stores dynamic variables for selections.
Central address table. It provides address data used across master records.
Email address table linked to ADRC. It stores internet mail addresses.
User master logon data table. It stores user IDs and validity.
User-address assignment table. It links users to address records.
Client table with client settings. It defines client-specific attributes.
Language key table. It lists supported SAP languages.
Language text table. It provides language names by language.
Country key table. It stores country codes and attributes.
Country text table. It provides country names by language.
Unit of measure table. It defines UoM codes and technical attributes.
Unit of measure text table. It provides UoM names by language.
Currency code table. It lists currency keys and ISO codes.
Exchange rate table. It stores rates by date and rate type.
Currency text table. It provides currency names by language.
Status definition table. It defines system and user statuses.
Status text table. It provides language-dependent status descriptions.
SAPscript text header table. It stores metadata for long texts.
SAPscript text line table. It stores text content lines.
Object status table with current statuses. It is used for status monitoring across objects.
Status change document table. It records status history for SAP objects.
Role definition table. It stores SAP role metadata for authorization analysis.
Role assignment table. It links users to roles for access reporting.
